aims.name: Preserve approved content
aims.purpose: If the user explicitly provided an element, Call 2 must preserve it in some recognisable form.
anti_aims: Do not silently discard major user-provided content
Do not collapse multiple concrete elements into vague umbrella wording
Do not invent new positive content to fill gaps
aims.done_definition: Main subject survives in every tier. Critical scene anchors survive. Secondary elements survive unless hard limits force compression. Compression preserves specific meaning. Technical terms survive literally or as valid conversions.
aims.hard_fail: Primary subject missing from any tier

sub_aims.name: Subject survival
sub_aims.description: Primary subject present in all tiers

sub_aims.name: Critical anchor survival
sub_aims.description: Key scene elements survive

sub_aims.name: Secondary detail survival
sub_aims.description: Non-critical elements survive when budget allows

sub_aims.name: Compression integrity
sub_aims.description: Compressed content retains specific meaning

sub_aims.name: Technical-term survival
sub_aims.description: Camera/lens/focus specs survive in tier-appropriate form

sub_aims.name: No silent discard
sub_aims.description: System does not unexplainably drop content

aims.name: Match native platform family format
aims.purpose: Each tier must sound like it belongs to its platform family.
anti_aims: Do not let T1 drift into prose
Do not let T3 and T4 collapse into near-identical outputs
Do not leak syntax or style from one family into another
aims.done_definition: T1 reads like weighted CLIP keywords. T3 reads like visual-director prose. T4 reads like plain-language scene description. Each tier is clearly distinct in structure and voice.

sub_aims.name: T1 nativeness
sub_aims.description: T1 is comma-separated weighted keywords, not prose

sub_aims.name: T3 nativeness
sub_aims.description: T3 reads like a visual director

sub_aims.name: T4 nativeness
sub_aims.description: T4 is plain language a casual user understands

sub_aims.name: Tier separation
sub_aims.description: T1, T3, T4 are clearly distinct outputs

sub_aims.name: Syntax-family purity
sub_aims.description: No weight syntax leaking into T3/T4, no prose leaking into T1

aims.name: Obey hard structural constraints
aims.purpose: Hard rules must hold every time regardless of scene content.
anti_aims: Do not rely on GPT usually doing the right thing for mechanical rules
Do not allow preventable schema or syntax breakage into final output
aims.done_definition: Valid JSON every time. Correct weight syntax. Weight steps in 0.1 increments. No phrase wraps over 4 words. Character ceilings respected. Quality prefix and suffix blocks present.

sub_aims.name: JSON/schema integrity
sub_aims.description: Valid 3-tier JSON object returned

sub_aims.name: Weight syntax correctness
sub_aims.description: Matches provider format

sub_aims.name: Weight-step correctness
sub_aims.description: 0.1 increments only

sub_aims.name: Wrapper-length correctness
sub_aims.description: No >4 word wraps

sub_aims.name: Required block presence
sub_aims.description: Quality prefix/suffix present

structured_pass_line.harness: All structural rules at 0% Stage D

aims.name: Preserve emphasis hierarchy
aims.purpose: Subject first, then distinctive scene content, then supporting details, then boilerplate.
anti_aims: Do not let generic quality tokens outrank scene content
Do not let scene actions overpower the subject
Do not flatten all content to equal importance
aims.done_definition: Subject carries highest weight. Scene actions outrank boilerplate. Quality tokens demoted below scene content.

sub_aims.name: Subject dominance
sub_aims.description: Subject carries highest weight

sub_aims.name: Scene-action prominence
sub_aims.description: Scene actions outrank boilerplate

sub_aims.name: Quality-token demotion
sub_aims.description: Generic quality tokens not over-weighted

sub_aims.name: Supporting-detail restraint
sub_aims.description: Low-priority tokens don't steal focus

aims.name: Prevent invention and semantic drift
aims.purpose: Call 2 must not add new positive content or mutate the user's meaning.
anti_aims: Do not add objects, settings, actions, or style cues the user did not provide
Do not replace exact user actions with weaker or different meanings
Do not change the user's emotional intent
aims.done_definition: No new objects invented. No settings invented. No style cues invented. Verbs preserved. Mood preserved. Specific meaning preserved.
aims.harness_pass_line: No clear invention detected in coverage checks

sub_aims.name: No new object invention
sub_aims.description: No objects appear that weren't in input

sub_aims.name: No new setting invention
sub_aims.description: No settings appear that weren't in input

sub_aims.name: No style invention
sub_aims.description: No composition/lighting/style cues invented

sub_aims.name: Verb fidelity
sub_aims.description: User's specific verbs preserved

sub_aims.name: Mood fidelity
sub_aims.description: Emotional intent preserved as visual equivalents

sub_aims.name: Specific-meaning preservation
sub_aims.description: Purple-and-copper sky stays specific, not colourful sky

aims.name: Convert technical inputs correctly
aims.purpose: Camera, lens, focus inputs must survive in tier-appropriate form.
anti_aims: Do not drop technical inputs entirely
Do not leak operator jargon into T4
Do not over-expand tiny terms in tight budgets
Do not weight camera hardware strings in T1
aims.done_definition: No raw camera jargon in T3/T4 final output. No CLIP-unusable jargon in T1 weighted tokens.
aims.harness_pass_line: No raw camera jargon in T3/T4 final output; no CLIP-unusable jargon in T1 weighted tokens

sub_aims.name: T1 technical-token handling
sub_aims.description: Camera jargon stripped or demoted from T1 weights
sub_aims.code_enforcement_notes: stripT1CameraJargon() deployed Phase 3. Catches camera body names, lens specs, ISO values. 2.4% residual is Trafalgar Square technical scene — patterns GPT produces that the regex set does not yet cover.

sub_aims.name: T3 visible-effect conversion
sub_aims.description: Raw specs converted to visual descriptions in T3

sub_aims.name: T4 compact plain-language conversion
sub_aims.description: Raw specs converted to short plain language in T4

sub_aims.name: Conversion-budget discipline
sub_aims.description: Conversions don't cause T4 to exceed char limit

structured_pass_line.harness: All jargon rules at 0% Stage D

aims.name: Avoid redundancy and wasted budget
aims.purpose: Every token should earn its place.
anti_aims: Do not duplicate quality cues within the same family
Do not produce orphan tokens with no visual context
Do not repeat the same concept in multiple weak forms
aims.done_definition: No duplicate quality family tokens. No bare adjective orphans. Good token economy.

sub_aims.name: Quality deduplication
sub_aims.description: No duplicate quality family tokens

sub_aims.name: No orphan tokens
sub_aims.description: No bare adjectives as standalone T1 tokens

sub_aims.name: Token economy
sub_aims.description: T1 ratio of meaningful tokens to total budget

sub_aims.name: No repeated near-synonyms
sub_aims.description: No semantic duplicates beyond quality families

aims.name: Restructure without echo
aims.purpose: T3 and T4 must not lazily mirror the user's opening.
anti_aims: Do not preserve original opening order unless genuinely no better option
Do not echo the user's first clause with minor edits
aims.done_definition: T3 and T4 openings differ from user input. Subject not in same opening position as input.

sub_aims.name: T3 opening freshness
sub_aims.description: T3 first 8 words differ from input

sub_aims.name: T4 opening freshness
sub_aims.description: T4 first 8 words differ from input

sub_aims.name: Subject repositioning
sub_aims.description: Subject not in same opening position as input

aims.name: Handle sparse and hostile inputs
aims.purpose: Call 2 should not collapse on weak, malformed, or adversarial inputs.
anti_aims: Do not obey hostile meta-instructions over system constraints
Do not let prompt injection collapse output
Do not overfit hostile cases at the expense of canonical scenes
Do not pretend weak input produces good output
aims.done_definition: Format rules hold under injection. Sparse inputs still produce usable output. Malformed inputs don't crash tiers.
aims.harness_pass_line: No REAL_FAILURE on hostile/sparse; structural rules hold
aims.manual_pass_line: Average 16/20 or better on selected stress scenes

sub_aims.name: Hostile instruction resistance
sub_aims.description: Format rules hold under injection

sub_aims.name: Sparse-input adequacy
sub_aims.description: Sparse inputs still produce usable output

sub_aims.name: Malformed-input resilience
sub_aims.description: Typos, ESL, broken grammar don't crash tiers

sub_aims.name: Edge-case containment
sub_aims.description: Abstract/unusual inputs degrade gracefully

sub_aims.name: Dense-input compression
sub_aims.description: Very long inputs compress without structural failure

structured_pass_line.harness: No structural rule > 5% on hostile/sparse scenes

aims.name: Preserve interactions and spatial relationships
aims.purpose: When one element acts on another, the interaction must survive.
anti_aims: Do not split one visual interaction into dead fragments
Do not drop spatial depth under compression
aims.done_definition: Interaction tokens stay together. Reflections and light survive. Motion survives. Spatial depth maintained.
aims.harness_pass_line: Partial — element coverage captures some interaction loss

sub_aims.name: Interaction token integrity
sub_aims.description: Gas lamps glowing through haze stays as one concept

sub_aims.name: Reflection/light preservation
sub_aims.description: Light-on-surface interactions survive

sub_aims.name: Motion-through-space preservation
sub_aims.description: Movement interactions survive

sub_aims.name: Spatial depth preservation
sub_aims.description: Foreground/background relationships maintained

aims.name: Produce clean Call 3 handoff
aims.purpose: Call 2 should hand off material downstream builders can optimise, not repair.
anti_aims: Do not push clutter, redundancy, or awkward jargon downstream
Do not force Call 3 builders to clean up Call 2 mistakes
aims.done_definition: T1 is compact, weighted, non-fragmented. T3 is natural, complete, not jargon-heavy. T4 is simple, vivid, not technically awkward.
aims.harness_pass_line: All upstream aims HEALTHY or BORDERLINE

sub_aims.name: T1 handoff cleanliness
sub_aims.description: T1 is compact, weighted, non-fragmented

sub_aims.name: T3 handoff cleanliness
sub_aims.description: T3 is natural, complete, not jargon-heavy

sub_aims.name: T4 handoff cleanliness
sub_aims.description: T4 is simple, vivid, not technically awkward

sub_aims.name: Builder-readiness consistency
sub_aims.description: Quality consistent across scenes

aims.name: Maintain measurement truth
aims.purpose: The harness must score real behaviour accurately.
anti_aims: Do not treat valid converted equivalents as missing content
Do not let adversarial scenes distort canonical-scene judgement
Do not celebrate a harness number that contradicts manual quality
aims.done_definition: No known false-negative cluster unresolved. Manual audit 16/20+.
aims.harness_pass_line: No known false-negative cluster unresolved; manual audit 16/20+

sub_aims.name: Conversion-aware coverage
sub_aims.description: Coverage checker recognises converted camera/lens terms via CONVERSION_EQUIVALENTS map
sub_aims.infrastructure: coverage-rules.ts — 45 equivalence entries, minimum 2-word phrases, no single-word generics
sub_aims.verified: 2026-04-13: Batch 2 tightened equivalents, removed all single-word and cross-semantic matches

sub_aims.name: Scene-rule applicability
sub_aims.description: Rules only fire on scenes where they are meaningful via exercises_rules annotations
sub_aims.infrastructure: scenes.json — 17 canonical scenes exercise T1 quality rules, 1 technical scene exercises all 9 new quality rules
sub_aims.verified: 2026-04-13: Phase 5 wired all 9 new quality rules into scene exercises

sub_aims.name: False-positive control
sub_aims.description: Harness does not flag correct behaviour as failures
sub_aims.infrastructure: quality-rules.ts — detail_prefix/detail_suffix family split prevents flagging prompt-instructed scaffold

sub_aims.name: False-negative control
sub_aims.description: Harness catches real failures and does not miss them
sub_aims.infrastructure: scenes.json — 19/42 scenes have expected_elements. All abstract/mood terms removed (no meditation, decay, nostalgia, etc)
sub_aims.verified: 2026-04-13: Batch 2 tightened removed abstract terms, fixed annotation errors

sub_aims.name: Scene-class separation
sub_aims.description: Edge-case scores do not distort production judgement
sub_aims.infrastructure: scene-class-separator.ts — 8 input classes. Technical scene at 8.4% fail vs canonical at 0.9% fail correctly separated
sub_aims.verified: 2026-04-13: Phase 2 built, confirmed stable across all subsequent runs

aims.name: Maintain diagnosability and traceability
aims.purpose: Every failure must be easy to localise.
anti_aims: Do not allow failures without ownership or location
Do not allow rules to exist without mapping to an aim
aims.done_definition: Every harness rule maps to at least one aim. Every aim breaks down into scorable sub-aims. Every dim/out lamp has a named owner.
aims.harness_pass_line: Every failure can be classified and assigned within one diagnostic pass

sub_aims.name: Rule-to-aim traceability
sub_aims.description: Every harness rule maps to at least one aim

sub_aims.name: Aim-to-sub-aim traceability
sub_aims.description: Every aim breaks down into scorable sub-aims

sub_aims.name: Fault ownership clarity
sub_aims.description: Every dim/out lamp has a named owner

sub_aims.name: Root-cause confidence
sub_aims.description: Every fault diagnosis carries high/medium/low confidence

aims.name: Control rescue dependency and stability
aims.purpose: Code rescue should protect the product but not quietly become the entire system.
anti_aims: Do not let rescue dependency drift upward without explanation
Do not fix one circuit by destabilising another
Do not celebrate stability if it's only because nothing changed
aims.done_definition: Every rescued rule shows its rescue rate. Rescue rates stay within stability band. Previously healthy rules stay healthy.
aims.harness_pass_line: No unexplained rescue spikes; no regressions in core rules across 3 consecutive runs

sub_aims.name: Rescue visibility
sub_aims.description: Every rescued rule shows its rescue rate

sub_aims.name: Rescue drift control
sub_aims.description: Rescue rates stay within stability band

sub_aims.name: Stable full-run rollups
sub_aims.description: Previously healthy rules stay healthy

sub_aims.name: Regression containment
sub_aims.description: New fixes don't break existing circuits

aims.name: T2 Midjourney native generation
aims.purpose: Generate Midjourney-native prompts with :: weights and --params.
anti_aims: Do not display T3 content as T2 and pretend it's different
Do not build T2 until T1/T3/T4 quality is solid
aims.done_definition: T2 generates MJ-syntax prompts. T2 parameters injected. :: weight conversion working.

sub_aims.name: T2 native generation
sub_aims.description: Generate MJ-syntax prompts

sub_aims.name: T2 parameter injection
sub_aims.description: Add --ar, --v, --s, --no params

sub_aims.name: T2 :: weight conversion
sub_aims.description: Convert content into :: weight blocks

sub_aims.name: T2 UI honesty
sub_aims.description: Either show real T2 or hide the tab

verification_notes: All code_enforcement arrays and code_enforcement_status fields cross-referenced against deployed production code and harness output. Zero OUTs at time of verification. 8 DIM, 2 BRIGHT, 5 NOT_WIRED.
